results.title: Top 10 Cuban Cigars and Their New World Alternatives: A Comparative Guide - Puroexpress Blog
results.content:
1. Cohiba Robusto vs. New World Rival

Cohiba Robusto: Known for its smooth and balanced flavor, this cigar is a staple for any aficionado.

New World Alternative: Rocky Patel Decade Robusto: Offers a similar medium to full-bodied experience with rich, complex flavors, often regarded as a top alternative for those seeking the Cohiba experience without the Cuban price tag.

2. Montecristo No. 2 vs. New World Rival

Montecristo No. 2: The torpedo-shaped cigar with a perfect balance of flavor and strength.  
 New World Alternative: My Father Le Bijou 1922 Torpedo: A rich, full-bodied cigar with deep, complex flavors, making it a strong contender against the Montecristo.

3. Partagas Serie D No. 4 vs. New World Rival [...] 9. Hoyo de Monterrey Epicure No. 2 vs. New World Rival

Hoyo de Monterrey Epicure No. 2: A classic Cuban robusto with a mild, creamy flavor.  
 New World Alternative: Merchant Cigars Toro: A premium cigar that offers a smooth, creamy experience, making it an excellent alternative for fans of the Hoyo de Monterrey.

10. La Gloria Cubana Medaille d’Or No. 4 vs. New World Rival

La Gloria Cubana Medaille d’Or No. 4: A slender, medium-bodied cigar with a complex flavor profile.  
 New World Alternative: Perdomo 20th Anniversary Sun Grown: This cigar offers a balanced, medium-bodied experience with a rich flavor profile, making it a worthy substitute. [...] 3. Partagas Serie D No. 4 vs. New World Rival

Partagas Serie D No. 4: This robusto is known for its rich, spicy flavor and perfect construction.  
 New World Alternative: Oliva Serie V Robusto: Offers a similarly rich experience with earthy, peppery notes that rival the Partagas in both flavor and quality.

4. H. Upmann No. 2 vs. New World Rival

H. Upmann No. 2: A classic Cuban with a smooth draw and mild flavor profile.  
 New World Alternative: Arturo Fuente Hemingway: A superbly crafted cigar with a smooth, creamy profile that can easily replace the H. Upmann in any humidor.

5. Romeo y Julieta Churchill vs. New World Rival

results.title: Cuban Cigar Alternatives | Holt's Cigar Company
results.content:
A hugely underappreciated cigar that is a great non-Cuban alternative to the D-4 is the Padrón 2000, 5 x 50 (about $6), from the original Padrón line. Padrón has made so many great cigars in the super-premium category that many forget that the original line is among the best, and best bargains available. This Nicaraguan cigar boasts some of the best construction and consistency in the industry. The tobacco is well aged. The cigar has a rich, earthy backbone and a good amount of complexity with cedar, nuts, leather, and cocoa coming through in abundance. A bonus is that this cigar comes in Natural or Maduro wrappers. [...] The slightly fuller-bodied non-Cuban alternative here is the San Cristobal Quintessence Corona Gorda, 5.625 x 46 (about $8). This cigar is Nicaraguan, made by the Garcia family, and rated 95 points with a ‘#3 Cigar of the Year’ ranking from Cigar Aficionado for 2021. The wrapper is Ecuador Habano, one of Pepin Garcia’s favorites to contain all-Nicaraguan filler. The Corona Gorda is a newer, slightly slimmer shape. The experience is one of extraordinary balance with all the components of flavor you expect from more powerful blends. The leather and spice yield to some sweetness and chocolate. The construction is about as good as it gets.

## Partagas Serie D No. 4 or Padron 2000 [...] My alternative for this Cuban cigar is the Oliva Serie V Lancero, 7 x 38 (at a remarkably un-whopping $8!), a Nicaraguan blend tucked under an Ecuador Habano wrapper. This cigar is a bit more intense than the Cuban Lancero, but it is supremely well balanced and smooth. Thinner cigars often provide a consistent dense smoke that can seem a bit stronger than it should be. I like to smoke them slowly, deliberately, letting the burn stay calm. The Oliva Lancero is earthy and, as you assimilate to the flavor, becomes nutty, with warm spices and good notes of chocolate. This cigar was named by Cigar Aficionado as one of the ‘Top 10 Cigars of 2019,’ so it might become a little difficult to find soon. Get some now!

## Montecristo No. 2 or the Ashton Estate Sun Grown (ESG) 22-Year Salute

results.title: Robusto Cigars: Size Guide, Flavor Impact & Top Recommendations
results.content:
Furthermore, the Robusto cigar ring size of 50 is substantial enough to feel satisfying in hand and mouth, yet not so large as to be cumbersome. This ergonomic design contributes to the overall enjoyment of the cigar, as it allows for a comfortable grip and a smooth draw. The Robusto’s popularity is also bolstered by its versatility; it can be enjoyed by both novice and experienced smokers, making it a universal favorite.

## Robusto vs other sizes

When comparing the Robusto cigar to other popular sizes, such as the Corona and Toro, several key differences emerge that highlight the unique attributes of each format.

### Robusto vs Corona [...] ### Robusto vs Toro

The Toro, on the other hand, is generally larger than the Robusto, measuring about 6 inches in length with a 50 to 54 ring gauge. The increased size of the Toro offers a longer smoking duration, typically between 60 and 90 minutes, which provides more time to enjoy the cigar’s evolving flavors. However, this extended smoking time may not appeal to everyone, especially those looking for a quicker smoke. The Robusto cigar size comparison to the Toro reveals that the Robusto is more concentrated, delivering a quicker, more intense burst of flavor that doesn’t require as much commitment in terms of time.

#### Robusto vs. other size chart [...] ## Is Robusto good for beginners?

A Robusto can be a great choice for beginners—but only when paired with the right blend. Strength in cigars comes from the tobacco composition, not the size, so a mild or medium-bodied Robusto is often ideal for those starting out.

What makes the Robusto especially beginner-friendly is its balanced format. With a typical 5 x 50 size, it offers a consistent ratio of wrapper, binder, and filler, resulting in a well-rounded flavor profile that is easier to understand compared to thinner or more complex formats.

results.title: Best 15 Robusto Cigars
results.content:
Top Lists

# Best 15 Robusto Cigars

Looking for the best robusto cigars? Here are 15 top robustos reviewed in detail. 45-70 minutes of smoke time. Thick enough for complex flavors. Short enough for everyday smoking. These fifteen robustos are all expert-reviewed with complete flavor profiles, construction notes, and third-by-third breakdowns.

If you want to know more about each cigar, click on the images to get to our detailed reviews.

Table of Contents

Toggle

## The 15 Best Robusto Cigars

### 1. Escobar Connecticut Robusto

Honeydew melon sweetness you won’t find anywhere else.

The construction’s almost too perfect—wrapper applied so cleanly it looks molded, not rolled. Incredibly firm and evenly rolled with silky, oily feel. [...] Expected 60 minutes. Got 80. Sometimes 3-4 minutes passed between puffs without it going out.

Well-balanced. Medium body.

### 5. PDR 1878 Capa Sungrown Robusto

90-minute robusto.

Firm and even construction across entire cigar. Oily wrapper. Pig’s tail and closed foot.

Ecuador wrapper. Dominican binder and filler.

Lovely and smooth complexity. Cool in the smoke. Can be smoked surprisingly slowly without going out or affecting taste. 90 minutes from a robusto is unusual.

Body is medium. Suits most aficionados.

### 6. PDR AFR-75 Cosecha Especial Shade Grown Robusto

Refined smoothness with complexity.

Surprisingly smooth with fresh flavor profile. Lovely complexity between thirds. Incredible balance throughout. [...] How to Properly Age Cigars: The Complete Guide
